changesProposed.name: Construct Capital

verified.name: Construct Capital
verified.thesis: Construct invests in foundational industries where disruptive technologies have not been applied despite decades of neglect. From manufacturing to logistics and defense to energy, these sectors are the backbone of GDP but have experienced chronic under-investment in innovation.
verified.notes: Founded year 2020 derived from source context mentioning 'Before leaving Uber in 2020, Rachel was...' and 'she co-founded Construct to expand this focus' with Rachel departing Uber in 2020. AUM not found in sources. Flagship fund name not found in sources. HQ location not explicitly stated in sources provided.

changesProposed.name: DCVC (Data Collective)

verified.name: DCVC
verified.thesis: DCVC backs entrepreneurs using AI to unlock potential across sectors. The firm invests in companies solving trillion-dollar problems at the intersection of computational approaches and massive datasets. Focus areas include AI, climate, engineering, materials science, robotics, space, water, biology, defense and security.
verified.notes: Source [1] states firm has been active for '15 years' as of content date and comprises '13 funds to date'. AUM listed as '$4b' in source [1]. Founded year cannot be verified from sources provided. HQ location not explicitly stated in sources. Flagship fund name not mentioned in sources. Sector focus areas are mentioned throughout but not in explicit sector list format. Sources indicate DCVC Bio established in 2018 as separate entity with four co-founding managing partners.

changes.current: DCVC backs entrepreneurs using AI to enable solutions across sectors. The firm invests at the intersection of computational approaches and massive datasets applied to trillion-dollar industries. DCVC focuses on deep tech solving trillion-dollar problems, with particular emphasis on climate, life sciences, robotics, space, water, biology, defense and security.
changes.verified: DCVC backs entrepreneurs using AI to unlock potential across sectors. The firm invests in companies solving trillion-dollar problems at the intersection of computational approaches and massive datasets. Focus areas include AI, climate, engineering, materials science, robotics, space, water, biology, defense and security.

changesProposed.name: Engine Ventures

verified.name: Engine Ventures
verified.thesis: Engine Ventures invests in early-stage Tough Tech companies developing hardware-based technologies across deep tech fields including biotech, robotics, green energy, agriculture, manufacturing, material science, and quantum computing. The firm spun out as a separate investment firm in 2023 from The Engine nonprofit incubator and accelerator.
verified.notes: Engine Ventures separated from The Engine nonprofit organization in 2023. The Engine is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit incubator/accelerator founded by MIT in 2016. Engine Ventures is the investment/VC arm. Current residents and former companies have collectively raised $5.5B in funding. No current senior partner names explicitly listed on firm website or Wikipedia for Engine Ventures specifically.

changes.current: Engine Ventures invests in Tough Tech companies commercializing breakthrough science and engineering. The firm supports early-stage deep tech startups developing hardware-based technologies across biotech, robotics, green energy, agriculture, manufacturing, material science, and quantum computing.
changes.verified: Engine Ventures invests in early-stage Tough Tech companies developing hardware-based technologies across deep tech fields including biotech, robotics, green energy, agriculture, manufacturing, material science, and quantum computing. The firm spun out as a separate investment firm in 2023 from The Engine nonprofit incubator and accelerator.

changesProposed.name: Harpoon Ventures

verified.name: Harpoon Ventures
verified.thesis: Harpoon invests in audacious founders building across AI, deep tech, software infrastructure, and cybersecurity. Founded in 2018 by a two-time Olympic medalist and former Navy SEAL, the firm operates on the belief that technology will define geopolitics.
verified.notes: AUM not explicitly stated in sources. Headquarters location (San Diego, CA) not found in provided sources. Flagship fund name not found in sources. Source [2] is Wikipedia article about harpoons (the weapon), not the venture capital firm, and is not relevant.

changesProposed.name: Shield Capital

verified.name: Shield Capital
verified.thesis: Shield Capital invests in early-stage companies building technologies that matter in artificial intelligence, autonomy, cybersecurity, and space. The firm's veteran team of founders, investors, and national security leaders focuses on the convergence of commercial technology and national security.
verified.notes: Founded year, AUM, flagship fund, and HQ location could not be verified from provided sources. SEC Form D filing (source 4) references 'Shield AI Inc' with different filing details, not clearly matching this firm. Key partners confirmed as current senior partners per team page (source 1): Philip Bilden and Raj Shah listed as 'Founder and Managing Partner', Michael Brown and John Serafini listed as 'Partner'.

changesProposed.name: Thrive Capital

verified.name: Thrive Capital Management, LLC
verified.hq: New York City, New York, U.S.
verified.thesis: Thrive Capital is a venture capital firm that focuses on software and internet investments. The firm invests across early-stage, growth, and late-stage companies while maintaining a strategy of investing heavily in fewer companies to provide closer relationships with founders. In November 2024, the firm remained faithful to early-stage investing while simultaneously participating in growth and late-stage investments.
verified.notes: In 2022, Nitin Nohria, former dean of Harvard Business School, became the firm's first executive chairman. Thrive announced in August 2024 that it raised over $5 billion for Thrive IX, split between a $4 billion late-stage investment fund and a $1 billion early-stage fund. As of 2026, Thrive Capital Partners X raised $10B+.

changes.verified: New York City, New York, U.S.

changes.current: Thrive Capital invests in software and internet companies. The firm has participated in notable investments across technology platforms and has expanded into growth and late-stage investing while maintaining early-stage investments.
